title: Agentic Project Manifest Schema
description: Manifest for agentic projects: products whose value is an orchestration of agents/subagents with skills, hooks, tools, and configurations — not traditional software, not academic/creative content. Examples: a corpus pipeline orchestrated by Claude Code + Claude Browser + Claude Desktop; a customer operations hub coordinating triage/drafter/escalation subagents; a compliance center monitoring regulatory feeds and classifying transactions.
$comment: Ratified empirically with 3 cases: exobase_med_corpus (8 roles, 4 state contracts, 8-stage pipeline), Customer Operations Hub (5 roles, hybrid lifecycle), Compliance Operations Center (6 roles, decisions_full observability). Roles are NOT a fixed enum — each project declares its own. The schema models the structural concepts that recur across agentic projects without imposing domain-specific values. additionalProperties:false at root forces consistency; sub-objects with operational metadata (rate_limits, etc.) allow additionalProperties:true for project-specific extensions.

project.description: Basic identity of the agentic project.
project.$comment: kind is a free string discriminator. Common values seen in the wild: 'pipeline' (ingestion/transformation), 'assistant' (interactive conversational), 'monitoring' (continuous surveillance + alert), 'orchestration' (high-level coordinator). Do NOT enforce an enum — the agentic paradigm is too young to lock these.

codename.description: Optional short codename for ecosystem-internal references.

description.description: High-level purpose of the agentic system: what it orchestrates, for whom, to achieve what outcome.

kind.description: Discriminator for the agentic project type. Free string — common values: 'pipeline', 'assistant', 'monitoring', 'orchestration', 'research'.
kind.$comment: Document your project's category here. Helps tooling and future readers identify the project shape at a glance.

orchestration.description: How the primary agent coordinates subagents: who talks to whom, communication protocol, write-ownership policy, approval gates.
orchestration.$comment: The core of an agentic project. primary_agent is the entity the human owner talks to. Subagents are dispatched by the primary. communication_protocol and write_ownership_policy are first-class because they determine whether the project can scale to many parallel subagents without race conditions.

primary_agent.description: The orchestrator that the human owner addresses directly.

role_ref.description: Reference to a role name declared in the top-level 'roles' array. The role definition carries tools, permissions, and constraints.

talks_to_owner.description: Whether this agent is the one the human owner interacts with. Typically true for the primary.

may_dispatch_roles.description: Names of roles the primary agent is allowed to dispatch. Empty/missing means it can dispatch any declared role.

communication_protocol.description: How the orchestrator and subagents exchange information.
communication_protocol.$comment: async_file_based: subagents write outputs to files, others read. Scales to many parallel subagents without locks when combined with single_writer_per_file. synchronous_inline: orchestrator waits for each subagent inline (simpler, lower parallelism). event_bus: pub/sub. custom: document elsewhere.

write_ownership_policy.$comment: single_writer_per_file: each file has exactly one writer (recommended for high parallelism — prevents collisions by construction, no locks needed). lock_based: explicit lock acquisition before write. append_only_log: writers only append, never modify. custom: document elsewhere.

approval_gates.description: Decisions that require approval (human or graduated condition) before the orchestrator executes them.

roles.description: All roles that subagents (and the primary) can play. Each project declares its own role names — there is no fixed enum.
roles.$comment: Roles encode responsibility + authorization. The pilot (exobase_med_corpus) declared 8 roles; Customer Ops Hub declared 5; Compliance Center declared 6. Each has its own domain semantics. tools_authorized and tools_forbidden create the security boundary: a role can only invoke tools it has authorization for.

lifecycle.description: ephemeral: subagents are spawned per task and die at completion (state lives in disk contracts). persistent: subagents live in background with their own state. hybrid: mix of both (e.g. a listener subagent is persistent while extractors are ephemeral).

logbooks_dir.description: Directory path where per-subagent logbooks live. Each subagent owns its own logbook file (single_writer_per_file principle).

skills.description: Reusable named procedures invokable by subagents.
skills.$comment: Skills are how repeatable procedures get a name and a version. Different from tools (which are infrastructure capabilities) — skills are domain-aware compositions. Example: 'extract-from-pubmed' is a skill that uses a browser tool + a parsing tool + a writer tool.

format.description: How skills are materialized on disk. markdown_with_frontmatter is the Claude Code default.

versioning_required.description: If true, every skill invocation must record its version in the output metadata. Enables retrospective invalidation of corpus when a skill's extraction logic changes.

tools.description: External capabilities (MCPs, APIs, browsers, filesystems, CLIs) available to roles. Roles reference tools by name in their tools_authorized list.

data_sources.description: Origins of input data the project consumes.

state_contracts.description: Persistent state structures shared across subagent invocations, governed by a JSON schema and a single writer role.
state_contracts.$comment: The single_writer_per_file principle materializes here. Each state contract has exactly one writer_role; everyone else reads. partitioning slices the contract into independently-writable files (e.g. one per subagent, one per specialty) for parallelism.

handoff_contracts.description: File-based handoffs between roles. Producer writes to file_pattern; consumer reads and processes.

pipelines.description: Processing pipelines with granular state transitions. Each transition is an atomic write — this is the primary resilience mechanism for crash recovery.

integration_contracts.description: Outputs delivered to downstream systems, with the shape contract that the downstream expects.

downstream.description: Name of the downstream system or package (e.g. 'facts_core', 'Slack', 'Postgres').

schema_ref.description: Optional reference to a JSON schema defining the contract shape.

data_shape.description: Concise description of what the data looks like and what the downstream expects.

triggers.description: How and when pipelines or roles get invoked.

governance.description: Boundaries, escape hatches, and budget controls.

owner_escape_hatch.description: Mechanism for the human owner to invoke a subagent directly, bypassing the orchestrator. Audited.

audit_log_path.description: File path where direct invocations are logged. Read by the orchestrator at session start.

scope_boundaries.description: Hard restrictions on what the system as a whole can do. Free strings — examples: 'no email sending', 'read-only on production DB', 'no destructive Postgres operations'.

daily_max_usd.description: Maximum total spend across all subagent calls per day.

observability.description: Logging and audit level for the project.

log_level.description: errors_only: only failures. errors_and_outputs: standard for MVP. decisions_full: every subagent decision logged (recommended for regulated domains like compliance). verbose: full trace including prompts.

audit_critical_decisions.description: If true, decisions tagged 'critical' by approval_gates produce a dedicated audit log entry regardless of log_level.

source_access_modes.description: Modes by which data sources are accessed in this project's domain. Free strings — each project defines its own.
source_access_modes.$comment: Pilot example: ['api', 'scraping', 'manual_handoff', 'institutional_negotiation']. Customer Ops example: ['mcp_api', 'webhook']. Compliance example: ['scraping', 'core_banking_stream']. Do NOT enforce an enum — modes are domain-specific.

role.description: A role that subagents (or the primary) play. Encodes responsibility and tool authorization.

name.description: Role name. Project-defined. Examples from real cases: 'orchestrator', 'browser_extractor', 'validator', 'indexer', 'state_updater', 'auditor', 'inspector', 'verifier' (pilot); 'ticket-triage', 'response-drafter', 'escalation-detector', 'kb-curator' (Customer Ops).

description.description: What this role is responsible for.

count_pattern.description: How many instances of this role exist. Free string — examples: '1' (singleton), 'N' (variable parallel count), '1_per_specialty', '1_per_jurisdiction', '1_per_source'.

tools_authorized.description: Names of tools (from top-level 'tools' array) this role is authorized to use.

tools_forbidden.description: Names of tools this role is explicitly forbidden from using. Useful for documenting security boundaries (e.g. 'indexer' is forbidden from 'browser' to prevent accidental scraping).

talks_to.description: Names of other roles this role may communicate with. Empty/missing means 'only orchestrator'.

writes_to.description: Paths or state_contract names this role is authorized to write. Establishes the single_writer_per_file boundary.

tool.description: An external capability available to roles.

name.description: Tool name. Referenced by roles in tools_authorized/tools_forbidden.

kind.description: mcp: MCP server. api: REST/GraphQL/gRPC client. browser: headless browser automation. filesystem: read/write local files. cli: shell command invocations. custom: document elsewhere.

requires_credentials.description: Whether this tool needs credentials (API key, OAuth token, etc.) configured separately.

access_mode.description: How the source is accessed. References values from top-level source_access_modes. Free string.

state_contract.description: A persistent state structure governed by a JSON schema.

schema_ref.description: Path to the JSON schema defining the contract structure.

volatility.description: stable: rarely changes (e.g. master catalog). volatile: changes per subagent invocation (e.g. progress trackers). ephemeral: exists per-extraction and gets consumed (e.g. extraction outputs handed off to indexer).

writer_role.description: Name of the role authorized to write this contract. Enforces single_writer_per_file by referencing one role only.

partitioning.description: How the contract is sliced into independently-writable files. Free string — examples: 'singleton' (one file), 'by_subagent' (one file per subagent), 'by_specialty', 'by_jurisdiction', 'by_severity'.

schema_ref.description: Optional JSON schema for the handoff payload.

pipeline_stage.description: One state in a pipeline's state machine. Transitions between stages are atomic writes — this is the resilience primitive.

role.description: Role responsible for transitioning out of this state.

transitions_to.description: Possible next states. A stage may have multiple outgoing transitions (e.g. 'validated' may transition to 'indexed' or 'rejected').

stuck_threshold_seconds.description: If a record stays in this state longer than this threshold without transitioning, a verifier role flags it as stuck.

trigger.description: How a pipeline or role gets invoked.

kind.description: manual: invoked by the owner. event: invoked by external event (webhook, file upload). schedule: invoked on a cron-like schedule. agent_cascade: invoked when another role completes.

spec.description: Trigger specification. For schedule: cron expression. For event: event name or webhook path. For agent_cascade: name of the upstream role whose completion triggers this. For manual: free description.

invokes_role.description: Role that gets invoked when this trigger fires.

approval_gate.description: A decision that requires approval before execution. Supports graduated approval via free-form auto_approve_when conditions.

action.description: What action is gated. Free string — examples: 'index_facts_to_db', 'send_response_to_customer', 'freeze_transaction', 'publish_compliance_alert'.

auto_approve_when.description: Free-form condition that, when true, allows the action to proceed without human approval. Examples: 'confidence > 0.9 AND volume < 100', 'severity != critical', 'always' (effectively no gate), 'never' (always requires human).

fallback.description: What happens when auto_approve_when is false. human_approval: surface to owner. block: refuse the action. log_only: log but do not execute.
